headline: The Single Premium Immediate Annuity That Adds $3,800 a Month to a $1.2 Million Income Portfolio Without Touching a Stock
summary: A 73-year-old retiree with a $1.2 million dividend portfolio generating $5,400 a month faces a quieter retirement problem than most. The income works, but it depends on corporations continuing to pay and raise dividends for another 20 to 25 years. Shifting $500,000 into a Single Premium Immediate Annuity (SPIA) replaces part of that market dependence ... The Single Premium Immediate Annuity That Adds $3,800 a Month to a $1.2 Million Income Portfolio Without Touching a Stock

headline: Quitting at 59 and Bridging Eight Years to Social Security at 67? Here Is the $530,000 Income Portfolio I Would Build
summary: For a 59-year-old hoping to leave work today, the math is unforgiving. A $530,000 brokerage account would need to generate $48,000 a year for the eight-year gap before Social Security begins at 67. That requires a yield of roughly 9%, well beyond what most sustainable income portfolios can produce without gradually eroding the principal that ... Quitting at 59 and Bridging Eight Years to Social Security at 67? Here Is the $530,000 Income Portfolio I Would Build

headline: HDV: This May Not Be The ETF You Want, But It Could Be The One You Need Right Now
summary: HDV offers defensive exposure with a 2.90% yield, focusing on quality, high-dividend companies with strong moats. Read more on HDV ETF here.

headline: What Procter & Gamble (PG)'s Restructuring, Cost Cuts, and Cash Returns Strategy Means For Shareholders
summary: In recent months, Procter & Gamble has launched new sensitive-skin products, advanced a major restructuring with up to 7,000 planned job cuts by fiscal 2027, and continued returning cash to shareholders through long-running dividend increases and buybacks. These moves highlight a company balancing investment in innovation and productivity with rising cost pressures, tariff headwinds of about US$400 million, and more cautious external earnings expectations. We’ll now examine how P&G’s...

headline: The Smartest Growth Stocks to Invest $10,000 in As Investors Rotate Out of Tech
summary: When investors take a risk-off attitude, stocks with attractive, growing dividends are often their safe-haven option.
